Value: During assembly, anti-seize contamination forms as sticky metallic and graphite-based deposits so tenacious adhesion builds up over time from heat and friction on surfaces. This contamination exhibits heat-resistant properties and strong bonding on steel, stainless steel, and aluminum, which distinguishes it from rust or oils because mechanical removal damages substrates during process. Key challenges occur in removal due to that bonding, so laser cleaning ablates layers precisely and base materials remain unharmed after treatment.
